seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Mastra + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Mastra + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Mastra enables real-time conversational AI through workflows that integrate speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech with sub-second latency. Guardrails are essential to prevent failures like malformed tool responses, stale memory context, and safety violations that can disrupt conversations or expose users to harmful content. Vex provides runtime verification as middleware to ensure reliability in this high-risk architecture.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Mastra
architectureOverview.description:
Mastra voice agents follow a streaming pipeline: real-time speech-to-text (e.g., Deepgram) captures audio, feeding transcripts into Mastra workflows for LLM reasoning with tools and memory. The workflow orchestrates agent steps, knowledge retrieval, and third-party integrations before generating TTS output (e.g., ElevenLabs). Sub-second latency demands synchronous processing with minimal blocking operations.

Key components include Mastra's AgentExecutor for tool calling, VectorStore for memory, and Workflow nodes for sequencing. Failures often occur at transitions between STT → reasoning → TTS, where unvalidated outputs or stale context cause hallucinations or delays. Vex middleware intercepts workflow steps to verify outputs against latency budgets and safety rules.

The pipeline emphasizes low-latency integrations and conversation state management across turns, making verification of coherence and appropriateness critical.

risks.name: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
risks.description: Unvalidated STT transcripts or LLM responses pass malformed data to TTS, causing garbled speech or infinite loops
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware schema validation on each Mastra workflow node output

risks.name: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
risks.description: External API tools return inconsistent JSON, breaking voice response formatting
risks.mitigation: Vex tool response verification with Pydantic-like schemas before workflow continuation

risks.name: Memory retrieval injecting stale context
risks.description: Vector store returns outdated conversation history, causing incoherent responses
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks and recency validation on retrieved context

risks.name: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
risks.description: Slow third-party APIs block the sub-second pipeline, injecting stale or partial data
risks.mitigation: Vex latency budgets and fallback mechanisms for integration responses
